APPEAL RECORD CERTIFIED TO THE COURT OF APPEAL - 1 VOL CT, 1 VOL RT (APPEAL FILED 5/17/12, A135546 DIV 3)
CERTIFICATION NOTICE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER DAVID KILLAM'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE COMPELLING DMV TO SET ASIDE SUSPENSION IS DENIED. RESPONDENT PROPERLY RELIED ON OFFICER CASTILLO'S UNSWORN NARRATIVE/SUPPLEMENTAL TO CONCLUDE THAT THE ADMONITION WAS GIVEN PRIOR TO THE BLOOD DRAW. (MACDONALD V. GUTIERREZ (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 150, 159; VEHICLE CODE 13557). ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)
AMENDED APPELLANT'S NOTICE DESIGNATING RECORD ON APPEAL FILED BY PETITIONER KILLAM, DAVID

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate Compelling Dmv To Set Aside Suspension
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Pam Herzig, Esq., of the Law Offices of Pam Herzig, Tel. 415.565.9600, appearing for Petitioner and moving party.
Terry Senne, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2118, appearing for Respondent and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER DAVID KILLAM's Petition For Writ Of Mandate Compelling DMV To Set Aside Suspension is Denied. Respondent properly relied on Officer Castillo's unsworn narrative/supplemental to conclude that the admonition was given prior to the blood draw. (MacDonald v. Gutierrez (2004) 32 Cal.4th 150, 159; Vehicle Code 13557). Order and Judgment signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
